ministry_department: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
short_description: Child health screening and early intervention services for children from birth to 18 years.
detailed_description: RBSK is a comprehensive program under National Health Mission to provide comprehensive health screening and early intervention services to all children from birth to 18 years. The program covers 4 'D's - Defects at birth, Deficiencies, Diseases, Development delays including disability. Free treatment and management is provided for identified health conditions through mobile health teams and referral services.
key_benefits: Free health screening for all children 0-18 years at schools and Anganwadi centers
Early detection of 30 selected health conditions including birth defects and disabilities
Free treatment and management of identified conditions at government facilities
Referral services to district and tertiary level hospitals for specialized care
Follow-up care and rehabilitation services for children with disabilities
other_conditions: Available to all children regardless of economic status
Screening conducted at schools, Anganwadi centers and health facilities
No registration or application required - automatic coverage
Mobile health teams visit regularly for screening
application_steps_offline: No application needed - screening teams visit schools and Anganwadi centers regularly
Parents can also take children to nearest Primary Health Center for screening
Mobile health teams conduct regular screening camps in communities
Identified cases are automatically referred for treatment
Follow-up is done by ASHA workers and health teams

submission_locations.address: Nearest PHC or Community Health Center
submission_locations.notes: RBSK mobile health teams visit schools and Anganwadis regularly for screening

processing_time: Immediate screening, treatment as per condition severity

faq.question: Is there any cost for screening and treatment?
faq.answer: No, all screening and basic treatment services under RBSK are completely free of cost.

faq.question: Where is screening conducted?
faq.answer: Screening is done at schools, Anganwadi centers, and health facilities by trained mobile health teams.

faq.question: What conditions are covered?
faq.answer: RBSK covers 30 health conditions including birth defects, childhood diseases, deficiencies, and developmental delays.

tips_for_successful_application: Ensure child attends school or Anganwadi regularly to not miss screening
Follow up on referrals for identified conditions promptly
Keep health records and referral slips provided by RBSK team
Contact ASHA worker or ANM for screening schedule
